rationale.root_cause: In CWE415_Double_Free__malloc_free_char_01_bad(), the same pointer is freed twice (around program.c:32 and program.c:34): the first free leaves data dangling, and the second free(data) triggers a double free.
rationale.local_fix: Delete the second free(data); so the allocation is freed exactly once (alternatively, null the pointer after the first free).
rationale.global_fix: Use a single-owner cleanup pattern with one responsible free site (e.g., a single exit path that frees data once), preventing multiple code paths from freeing the same allocation.
